vectorDB.text: Here is the English translation of the text, structured for clear readability: If a partnership is handled systematically, it can grow two or three times larger than a solo venture. However, if it lacks structure, the troubles will also multiply by two or three times. First, I would like to share the most common issues among the reasons why partnership businesses fail. 1. Two Lions in One Cave The first point is that there will always be problems if two lions try to live in the same cave. In ventures formed by brilliant leaders where everyone is highly capable, the idea of two people trying to lead equally is quite dangerous. While there might not be any issues initially, friction will inevitably arise as you work together over time. Eventually, these interpersonal clashes can end up damaging the business itself. 2. Lack of Clear Boundaries and Responsibilities The second point is the failure to clearly divide responsibilities and decision-making boundaries when working together. This is the number one cause of problems between partners. Even if both of you are going to be leaders, this must absolutely be taken into account. Based on your differing strengths, you must establish a mutual agreement from the very beginning regarding who is responsible for which area and who has the final say on specific matters. Without these clear boundaries, everyday operations will lead to misunderstandings—for instance, if one partner makes a decision, the other might start thinking, "Are they bypassing me?" 3. Written Over Verbal Agreements The final and most important point is that whatever you do, all agreements should be made in writing rather than relying on verbal promises. As the saying goes, "A hundred spoken words cannot match a single written word." The power of a written document is far stronger and more reliable than spoken words when issues inevitably arise. Because you have tangible proof, resolving any disputes becomes significantly easier. In conclusion: When partnerships fail, it is usually not because the business idea itself was bad, but rather because of these underlying frictions and minor problems between the partners.

Brand Guideline Illicit: Naught but authentic self Step into the world of "illicit ", a local clothing brand that offers a variety of fashionable accessories and artistic apparels for those who seek to express their unique individuality. It all started in 2018. In a sea of clothing brand in casual fits, we saw a lack of originality in Myanmar fashion industry that left customers yearning for more than worn out and cookie cutter styles in their Daily life. We set out to fill this gap by offering a refreshing alternative that combines affordable artistic flair. It's our belief that fashion is a medium for self expression and each individual deserves to stand out among the crowd without falling pit of unoriginality. To arrive this end, we took a daring step to reinvent casual apparels and other accessories infusing the simplicity with artistically unique designs that resonate with one's own personal identity. We forge our products with passion and commitment to reflect your inner uniqueness. " Illicit; serve as a bridge spanning you and your timeless authentic self " Products PRODUCT NAME: Bon Kan Nway SLOGAN: Descendants of Rebels DESCRIPTION: Bon Kan Nway is a tunic made from Inlay Shan cotton. Its design is a homage to previous revolutions in Burma. The back of the garment features newsprint stories from the1962 student uprisings. The tag on the sleeve features handwriting from a present-day activist, Tayzar San, and highlight significant dates across Myanmar's political history. The Bon Kan Nway tunic serves as a symbol of multi-generational struggle against military dictatorship. PRODUCT NAME: Trauma SLOGAN: "The flowers that remain after all we have endured" DESCRIPTION: From the start we mean the trauma as an individual pain of us. But since the coup, it became an expression of the pain and hope for a brighter future of the whole nation. Even we had worst experience, we still resisted and survived. PRODUCT NAME: JAPANESE INSPIRED CARDIGAN DESCRIPTION: Where simplicity meets style, this comfort casual shirt from illicit is designed for those who appreciate effortless fashion. Crafted from premium combed cotton sourced from Shan State, Myanmar, it delivers unmatched comfort and quality. As a tribute to Myanmar's rich heritage, the shirt features elegant patchwork made from traditional Burmese longyi, blending cultural significance with modern design. Perfect for those who value being fashionable yet simple, this shirt embodies timeless style with a unique cultural touch. PRODUCT NAME: ILLICIT COMFORT CASUAL SHIRT LESS IS MORE LOGO PRINTED SHIRT DESCRIPTION: Where simplicity meets style, this comfort casual shirt from illicit is designed for those who appreciate effortless fashion. Crafted from premium combed cotton sourced from Shan State, Myanmar, it delivers unmatched comfort and quality. Perfect for those who value being fashionable yet simple, this shirt embodies timeless style with a unique minimal touch. ILLICIT COMFORT CASUAL SHIRT LESS IS MORE LOGO PRINTED SHIRT DESCRIPTION: Where simplicity meets style, this comfort casual shirt from illicit is designed for those who appreciate effortless fashion. Crafted from premium combed cotton sourced from Shan State, Myanmar, it delivers unmatched comfort and quality. Perfect for those who value being fashionable yet simple, this shirt embodies timeless style with a unique minimal touch. PRODUCT NAME: LAZY SUMMER DAYS TEE DESCRIPTION: This tee was born from the emptiness we felt during the ca time when the future seemed lost, and we had no energy to move forward. It speaks to that quiet struggle, where summer doesn't bring joy or hope, just a feeling of inertia. We keep moving, not because we expect better, but because we have to. Simple, honest, and raw, this tee is for everyone who carries the weight of hard times but keeps going anyway-a reminder that even in the darkest moments, we endure.

Si Thu Aung is a 27 yrs old male from Myanmar who graduated with Business Administration from Kasem Budit University. He was one of the top students and got scholarship for 4 years. He also played for KBU Futsal Team in 2017 – 2018 seasons, but now he played in Zinme United from Chiang Mai, Thailand.
